description: Close the three structural gaps blocking hex from running workplans end-to-end without Claude Code as the outer shell. Wire a second AgentManager composition path, ship OllamaInferenceAdapter as the first concrete standalone provider, un-stub the HexFlo memory adapter, and add a standalone-mode acceptance gate to hex ci. The Claude-integrated fast path is preserved; standalone becomes the documented fallback.
blocks: Every AIOS positioning claim that asserts hex runs without Claude

phases.name: Port surface for standalone inference
phases.description: Define the IInferencePort trait surface that OllamaInferenceAdapter (P3) and the reworked ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter (P4) will implement. Must expose: (1) complete(prompt, params) -> Result<Response>, (2) stream(prompt, params) -> Result<TokenStream>, (3) health() -> Result<HealthStatus>. No concrete adapter yet — this phase is pure interface + unit-testable mock. Domain types live in hex-core; port trait lives in hex-core::ports. Audit current hex-nexus/src/adapters/inference* to identify any ad-hoc trait that is half-formed and either adopt it or replace it. Do NOT leave two competing traits.

tasks.name: Audit existing inference trait surface
tasks.description: Read every adapter file under hex-nexus/src/adapters/inference* and hex-core/src/ports. Write a one-page notes file docs/analysis/inference-trait-audit.md listing: (a) what traits exist, (b) what methods each defines, (c) where the Claude-code-shaped assumptions are (e.g. 'spawns a subprocess', 'reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID'), (d) recommended consolidation. Output feeds P1.2.

tasks.name: Define canonical IInferencePort in hex-core
tasks.description: REFINE-IN-PLACE (not a rewrite). P1.1 audit found IInferencePort already exists in hex-core/src/ports/inference.rs with complete/stream/capabilities and zero Claude-specific assumptions in the trait itself. P1.2 scope: (1) add health() -> Result<HealthStatus, InferenceError> as a new async method matching the existing async_trait convention, (2) add HealthStatus enum (Ok { models }, Degraded { reason }, Unreachable { reason }) in the same module, (3) create MockInferencePort in hex-core/src/ports/inference/mock.rs for P2/P5 test use (pure in-memory, no feature flag since hex-core has no cargo features yet), (4) implement health() on the one existing impl — SpacetimeInferenceAdapter in hex-agent/src/adapters/secondary/spacetime_inference.rs — because adding a trait method is breaking. No ad-hoc inference traits exist to delete (audit §6). Gate widened from `cargo check -p hex-core -p hex-nexus` to `cargo check --workspace` per audit §7 gotcha #2 so the hex-agent impl is covered.

phases.name: Standalone composition path for AgentManager
phases.description: Add a second composition variant that constructs AgentManager from (HexFlo coordination layer + IInferencePort + IHexFloMemoryStatePort) without reading ~/.hex/sessions/agent-{CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}.json. The existing Claude-integrated path stays as the preferred branch; the standalone branch fires when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset AND no agent-*.json session file is present. The executor's 'AgentManager not initialized' error at workplan_executor.rs:746-750 is replaced with a structured diagnostic naming which prerequisite is missing (inference adapter, HexFlo reachability, or port wiring). Unit tests use MockInferencePort from P1.2.

tasks.name: Standalone composition root variant
tasks.description: Add hex-nexus/src/composition/standalone.rs with fn compose_standalone(cfg, inference: Arc<dyn IInferencePort>) -> AgentManager. Wires HexFlo + inference + memory adapter into the same AgentManager type the Claude-integrated path produces. The existing compose() dispatches between the two variants based on env probe. Do NOT duplicate Claude-specific code into the standalone path — share everything except the agent-identity source.

tasks.name: Replace AgentManager init error with structured diagnostic
tasks.description: At hex-nexus/src/orchestration/workplan_executor.rs:746-750, replace the 'AgentManager not initialized' string error with a typed MissingComposition { missing: Vec<Prerequisite> } error that names exactly which piece is absent. Error Display impl prints a remediation hint ('run `hex doctor composition` to diagnose'). Propagate through the executor's phase error path.

tasks.name: Unit tests for standalone composition
tasks.description: Add hex-nexus/tests/composition_standalone.rs. Cases: (a) CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset + MockInferencePort -> standalone branch used, AgentManager populated. (b) CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set -> Claude-integrated branch used. (c) standalone branch + MissingComposition when inference port is absent -> structured error, not string. Tests run with HexFlo pointed at an in-memory fixture (not live SpacetimeDB) so CI is hermetic.

phases.name: OllamaInferenceAdapter — first concrete standalone provider
phases.description: Implement IInferencePort (P1.2) against a local Ollama HTTP endpoint. POST /api/generate + /api/chat + /api/tags for health. Respect OLLAMA_HOST env var (default http://localhost:11434). Stream tokens via Ollama's NDJSON response format. Unit tests use httpmock (or wiremock) to simulate Ollama; no live Ollama required in CI. The adapter is the reference implementation — it must be tight enough that adding OpenAI / vLLM later is a matter of copy+swap, not copy+rewrite.

tasks.name: OllamaInferenceAdapter complete()
tasks.description: hex-nexus/src/adapters/inference/ollama.rs: struct OllamaInferenceAdapter { base_url, client: reqwest::Client }. Implements IInferencePort::complete by POST /api/generate with { model, prompt, stream: false }. Error handling: connection refused -> Unreachable, 404 model -> UnknownModel, 5xx -> Upstream. Honor adapter-level timeout (default 120s, configurable via OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECS).

tasks.name: OllamaInferenceAdapter stream()
tasks.description: Implement IInferencePort::stream against /api/generate with stream: true. Parse NDJSON line-by-line, yield TokenChunks via a futures::Stream. Cancellation: dropping the stream closes the HTTP connection. Backpressure: bounded channel capacity 64.

tasks.name: OllamaInferenceAdapter health() + wiring
tasks.description: Implement health() by GET /api/tags and return HealthStatus::Ok { models: Vec<String> } or HealthStatus::Unreachable on connection failure. Wire the adapter into the standalone composition path from P2.1 as the default when OLLAMA_HOST is reachable. If not reachable, composition returns MissingComposition { inference: 'ollama: connection refused' } — no silent fallback.

tasks.name: Mocked Ollama integration test
tasks.description: hex-nexus/tests/ollama_adapter.rs: spin up httpmock server that mimics Ollama /api/generate, /api/chat, /api/tags. Cases: (a) happy-path complete, (b) streaming yields N tokens in order, (c) 404 unknown model, (d) connection refused -> Unreachable, (e) stream cancellation closes the connection. No live Ollama dependency.

phases.name: Rework ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter as a backend, not a shell
phases.description: Today ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter (if it exists) assumes Claude Code is the outer shell; it reads session state and leans on hooks for dispatch. Rework it into a plain IInferencePort implementation that spawns `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions` as a subprocess backend, same status as OllamaInferenceAdapter. The Claude-integrated fast path continues to bypass this adapter when CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is present (P2.1 chooses composition, not this adapter). Fixes the known `claude -p` exit-1 bug from feedback_claude_bypass_permissions memory.

tasks.name: Audit and isolate the current ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter
tasks.description: Grep for ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter across hex-nexus. Document what it currently does in docs/analysis/claude-code-adapter-audit.md. If it doesn't exist yet, note that and design it fresh. Either way, identify every place in the codebase that reads CLAUDE_SESSION_ID and classify each as 'composition concern' (stays) or 'adapter concern' (moves into the adapter or gets deleted).

tasks.name: ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter rewrite (or creation)
tasks.description: hex-nexus/src/adapters/inference/claude_code.rs: implements IInferencePort by spawning `claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions <prompt>` via tokio::process::Command. ALWAYS passes --dangerously-skip-permissions when not attached to a TTY (fixes exit 1, per feedback_claude_bypass_permissions). complete() captures stdout to completion; stream() reads stdout line-by-line as tokens. health() runs `claude --version`. Does NOT read CLAUDE_SESSION_ID or session files — that's composition's job.

tasks.name: ClaudeCodeInferenceAdapter tests with mocked subprocess
tasks.description: Inject a process spawner trait so tests can feed canned stdout/exit codes without a real claude binary. Cases: (a) happy path captures full response, (b) exit 1 without --dangerously-skip-permissions flag is impossible (flag is unconditional) — assert flag presence in command line, (c) binary missing -> Unreachable, (d) stream yields tokens in order.

phases.name: Un-stub HexFlo memory adapter (spacetime_state.rs)
phases.description: Replace Err(Self::err()) stubs in hex-nexus/src/adapters/spacetime_state.rs::hexflo_memory_{store,retrieve,search,delete} with real SpacetimeDB reducer calls against the hexflo-coordination module. Reducers already exist in spacetime-modules/hexflo-coordination/src/lib.rs — this phase is pure adapter wiring, no new reducers. SQLite fallback path (ADR-011) remains for offline mode. If the reducer name / signature is missing for any of the four methods, add it to the WASM module in the same phase rather than split across workplans.

tasks.name: Reducer inventory + gap analysis
tasks.description: Read spacetime-modules/hexflo-coordination/src/lib.rs. List the existing reducers that cover memory_store / memory_retrieve / memory_search / memory_delete. If any are missing or under-specified, document the gap in docs/analysis/hexflo-memory-reducer-gap.md and decide whether to extend the WASM module (in P5.2) or work around it in the adapter.

tasks.name: Extend hexflo-coordination reducers if needed
tasks.description: Only runs if P5.1 identifies missing reducers. Adds/extends spacetime-modules/hexflo-coordination/src/lib.rs. Republishes the module. Regenerates bindings in hex-nexus/src/adapters/spacetime_bindings/. If P5.1 finds all four reducers are already sufficient, this task is skipped and marked `done` with result 'no reducer changes needed'.

tasks.name: Implement hexflo_memory_* adapter methods
tasks.description: Replace Err(Self::err()) bodies in hex-nexus/src/adapters/spacetime_state.rs. Each method calls the corresponding reducer, maps SpacetimeDB errors to domain error types, and returns structured results. Add instrumentation (tracing::debug spans) so failures are diagnosable without attaching a debugger. Adapter-level tests use an in-memory SpacetimeDB fixture (or the existing test harness if one exists — grep hex-nexus/tests/ first).

tasks.name: End-to-end hexflo_memory test via REST
tasks.description: hex-nexus/tests/hexflo_memory_e2e.rs: start hex-nexus against an in-memory SpacetimeDB fixture, POST /api/hexflo/memory to store, GET /api/hexflo/memory/:key to retrieve, GET /api/hexflo/memory/search?q= to search, DELETE to remove. Asserts the full CLI -> REST -> adapter -> reducer path works. This test exists to prove the un-stubbing landed end-to-end, not just at the unit level.

phases.name: Executor dispatch through standalone composition (end-to-end wiring)
phases.description: With P2 (composition), P3 (Ollama), P4 (Claude-as-backend), and P5 (memory) in place, wire workplan_executor to dispatch through the standalone AgentManager. A phase with one task in a workplan must produce a real inference call (via MockInferencePort in tests, OllamaInferenceAdapter when run manually), persist progress to HexFlo memory, and surface the result through the existing /api/workplan/execute REST path. The dispatch-evidence guard (ADR-2604111800) must accept the run — its rejection is the signal that we are not cheating.

tasks.name: Standalone dispatch path in workplan_executor
tasks.description: At the point where workplan_executor calls AgentManager.dispatch_task (grep to find the exact line post-P2), ensure the standalone AgentManager routes to IInferencePort rather than expecting a Claude session. The code path should be indistinguishable at the executor level — the composition root already chose the right AgentManager variant. This task is mostly validation that nothing in the executor reaches past the AgentManager abstraction to read session files directly.

tasks.name: One-task-workplan end-to-end test (MockInference)
tasks.description: hex-nexus/tests/standalone_dispatch_e2e.rs: construct a minimal wp-*.json with one phase and one task. Boot hex-nexus in standalone composition with MockInferencePort returning canned code. Call /api/workplan/execute. Assert: (a) task transitions todo -> in_progress -> done, (b) dispatch-evidence guard accepts the completion (ADR-2604111800), (c) task result contains mock output, (d) HexFlo memory has a record of the dispatch.

tasks.name: Reject vacuous completions — explicit test of the guard
tasks.description: In the same test file, add a case where MockInferencePort returns empty / whitespace output. Assert the dispatch-evidence guard (ADR-2604111800) rejects the completion with the documented error, NOT a phantom 'done'. This test exists so the guard contract is proven against the new composition path, not just the old one.

phases.name: Standalone acceptance gate in hex ci + doctor check
phases.description: The ADR's guardrail: `hex ci` must run a one-task workplan end-to-end on a host where CLAUDE_* env vars are unset and no Claude CLI is installed. This proves the gap is closed and keeps it closed. Also add `hex doctor composition` that diagnoses which composition prerequisites are present / missing, so operators have a fast triage command.

tasks.name: hex doctor composition check
tasks.description: Add a DoctorCheck in hex-cli/src/commands/doctor/ that probes: (a) is CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set?, (b) does ~/.hex/sessions/agent-*.json exist?, (c) is OLLAMA_HOST reachable?, (d) is the `claude` binary on PATH?, (e) is hex-nexus reachable?. Reports each as PASS/FAIL/WARN and prints which composition variant would be selected. Run via `hex doctor composition` or as part of `hex doctor --all`.

tasks.name: hex ci --standalone-gate
tasks.description: Add a --standalone-gate flag to `hex ci` that: (a) unsets CLAUDE_* env vars in a scoped subshell, (b) spins up an httpmock Ollama server on a free port and exports OLLAMA_HOST, (c) boots hex-nexus in standalone composition, (d) runs the one-task workplan from P6.2 (reused as a fixture, NOT copy-pasted), (e) asserts exit code 0 + task status done + dispatch-evidence guard happy. This is the acceptance gate the ADR declares. Fails fast with a clear message if any step regresses.

tasks.name: Document the standalone mode in README + CLAUDE.md
tasks.description: Add a 'Running hex standalone (without Claude Code)' section to the root README.md (short — just the three commands: install Ollama, hex nexus start, hex plan execute). Update CLAUDE.md to mention that ADR-2604112000 established the standalone composition path, so future workplans can rely on it. Do NOT document it as experimental — once P7.2 passes, it's a first-class mode.

tasks.name: Final workplan review via adversarial-reviewer
tasks.description: Run the adversarial-reviewer agent against the full wp-hex-standalone-dispatch change set before marking status=done. Inputs: every file modified by P1-P7, the ADR text, and the audit report findings. Looks for: (a) dangling refs to the old composition path, (b) stale test harnesses, (c) hex-intf-specific leakage in anything added under hex-cli/assets/ (per ADR-2604111142), (d) reducer name drift between WASM module and adapter bindings. Blocking: any HIGH severity finding halts the workplan.
